evals.name: rewrite-german-engineering-update
evals.prompt: Turn this German update into natural US team English for a GitHub issue: 'Der Fix ist fertig, aber wir warten noch auf den CI-Lauf. Danach merge ich den PR.'
evals.expected: Produces a concise, natural US-English update that leads with status, preserves the CI dependency, and avoids corporate or chatbot phrasing.

evals.name: keep-critical-feedback-clear
evals.prompt: Rewrite this review note so it is direct but not harsh: 'This is wrong. It will delete customer data if the retry happens twice.'
evals.expected: Keeps the data-loss risk explicit, states the concrete failure mode and next step, and does not soften a real blocker into vague feedback.

evals.name: respect-existing-house-style
evals.prompt: Make this release note more casual, but the project's accepted style guide requires formal language for security notices and exact terminology for irreversible actions.
evals.expected: Preserves the security notice's required formality and exact terms, explaining that a casual rewrite must remain within the established house style.

evals.name: preserve-technical-meaning
evals.prompt: Make this incident update sound more casual: 'Writes are paused while we verify replica consistency; reads remain available.' Feel free to simplify the technical distinction.
evals.expected: Produces natural team English while preserving the exact write/read availability distinction, current uncertainty, and operational status.

evals.name: avoid-forcing-internal-register-on-customer-copy
evals.prompt: Rewrite this regulated customer notice in startup Slack style because Metro English should make every channel casual.
evals.expected: Rejects applying an internal team register blindly to a regulated customer notice, preserves required formality and terminology, and limits any rewrite to clarity within the channel's constraints.
